semanticCore: Slander, tale-bearing, whispers to create division, disclosing secrets maliciously, or small bugs

keyDerivations.meaning: Slander, tale-bearing, whispering malicious reports to damage relations

figurativeOrRareUsages: هَمَّازٍ مَشَّاءٍ بِنَمِيمٍ (68:11): Defamer, going about with slander/tale-bearing (describing Al-Walid ibn al-Mughirah's character).
summaryEn: The root represents tale-bearing and malicious whispering (Namm), condemning those who walk around distributing gossip to sabotage trust within the community.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر السعاية بالحديث والوشاية ونقل الكلام بين الناس لقصد الإفساد وتفريق القلوب (نمم)، ذامّاً خصلة النمام الساعي بين المؤمنين بالفتنة.
rawText: مُنَمْنَمٌ A garment, or piece of cloth, figured with marks resembling writing, or otherwise. See مَذَبَّرٌ. نَامَّةٌ The artery in the head: see أَبْهَرُ. نَمَانِمُ The sutures of the skull; as resembling lines of writing; see شَأْنٌ. نَمِيمَةٌ The sound of the bow-string. (Kr.) === نَمِيمَةٌ Malicious and mischievous misrepresentation; calumny; slander; (S, K;) the embel-lishment of speech with falsehood. (Kr.) نَمَّ 1 He [uttered calumny: or] excited discord, or dissension, and made known discourse in a mischievous manner; or embellished speech with falsehood. (M, K.) You say, نَمَّ بِهِ and عَلَيْهِ, inf. n. نَمٌّ and نَمِيمَةٌ and نَمِيمٌ, or this last is pl. of نَمِيمَةٌ, (M,) meaning, He calumniated him; or misrepresented him. === نَمَّ الحَدِيثَ He made known, divulged, or told, conversation, in a malicious or mischievous manner, so as to occasion discord, dissension, or the like. (TA, art. قت; and Msb. *) نَمَّامٌ now commonly applied to Wild thyme, thymus serpyllum: see شَبَهَانٌ. نَمْنَمَ He variegated a thing: he decorated or embellished it. (S, K.)
